name: Turkey (the bird)
description: Wild and domesticated turkeys, gobbles, and Thanksgiving lore.

questions.q: Which scientific name designates the wild turkey?

source.notes: Meleagris ocellata is the ocellated turkey of the Yucatán.

questions.q: How many living species of turkey are there?

source.notes: Wild turkey (M. gallopavo) and ocellated turkey (M. ocellata).

questions.q: What is a baby turkey called?
What is a male turkey called?
What is a female turkey called?
What is the dangling, fleshy red flap of skin under a turkey's chin called?

source.notes: The 'snood' is the fleshy bit that hangs over the beak; the 'caruncle' is the bumpy red skin on neck.

questions.q: Which Founding Father reportedly preferred the turkey to the bald eagle as the U.S. national bird?

source.notes: Mentioned in his 1784 letter to his daughter — but he never formally proposed it for the seal.

questions.q: Which U.S. president began the modern tradition of pardoning a Thanksgiving turkey?

source.notes: The first official pardon was in 1989. Lincoln spared a turkey for his son Tad in 1863, but it wasn't an annual ceremony.

questions.q: Where were turkeys first domesticated, around 2,000 years ago?

source.notes: Indigenous Mesoamericans domesticated wild turkeys ~800 BCE - 100 BCE.

questions.q: Why is the bird (Meleagris gallopavo) called 'turkey' in English?
choices: It crosses the Bosporus during migration
It was named after Sultan Suleyman
Mistaken trade route confusion with Turkish guinea fowl

source.notes: Europeans confused American turkeys with similar-looking guinea fowl imported via Ottoman 'Turkey merchants.'

questions.q: On which Thursday is U.S. Thanksgiving — when most turkeys are eaten — celebrated?
Approximately how many turkeys are eaten in the U.S. on Thanksgiving Day?
Which amino acid in turkey is famously (incorrectly) blamed for post-Thanksgiving sleepiness?

source.notes: Turkey doesn't contain more tryptophan than other meats — the post-meal sleepiness is more about overeating carbs.

questions.q: Roughly how fast can a wild turkey run?

source.notes: Wild turkeys can run ~25 mph and fly short distances at ~55 mph.

questions.q: Can wild turkeys fly?
choices: Yes, for hundreds of miles in migration

source.notes: They roost in trees at night. Domestic turkeys, bred to be heavy, can't fly.

questions.q: Which Looney Tunes Southern-drawling cartoon rooster has the iconic Texas-style 'big mouth' voice?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: corrected — Foghorn is a rooster (leghorn chicken), not a turkey. His Southern barnyard bird-humor style is iconic.

questions.q: Which 1967 Arlo Guthrie spoken-word holiday classic became a Thanksgiving radio tradition?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: replaced incorrect '1972 Bowery Hill' framing — Arlo Guthrie's 'Alice's Restaurant Massacree' was released October 1967; events set in Stockbridge, Massachusetts. A Thanksgiving radio tradition.

questions.q: What do you call a group of turkeys?

source.notes: A 'rafter' or sometimes a 'gang' of turkeys.

questions.q: Which U.S. state is the largest turkey producer?

source.notes: Minnesota produces ~40M+ turkeys/year — the most of any state.

questions.q: What sound does a male turkey famously make to attract mates?

source.notes: Only males gobble; females make clucks and yelps.

questions.q: What color is the head and neck skin of a male wild turkey when it is excited or displaying?
Which holiday in the United States is most associated with eating turkey?
What is the long, stiff cluster of feathers that sticks out from the chest of a turkey called?
On which continent is the turkey native in the wild?
Roughly how much can a large domesticated male turkey weigh?
The fleshy growth that hangs over a turkey's beak, especially noticeable in males, is called the what?
What is the white meat that most people prefer from a roasted turkey?
What is the wishbone of a turkey traditionally used for?
choices: Making a wish by pulling it apart

questions.q: Turkeys are members of the same bird family as which common farm bird?
What do you call the act of a male turkey fanning out his tail feathers to impress females?
Which of these is a popular brand of frozen and packaged turkey products in the U.S.?
What is the name for the fleshy, bumpy growths along a turkey's neck and head?
Roughly how fast can a wild turkey fly over short distances?
Where do wild turkeys typically sleep at night?
choices: In tall grass on the ground

questions.q: The ocellated turkey, a separate species from the common turkey, is native to which region?
Turkeys have excellent vision; roughly what field of view can they see?
choices: A full 360 degrees with no head movement

questions.q: What is the small, sharp, spur-like projection on the back of a male turkey's leg used for fighting?
By the mid-20th century wild turkeys had nearly vanished from much of the U.S.; what largely brought their populations back?
choices: A federal ban on eating turkey

questions.q: Which 16th-century explorer's expeditions are commonly credited with helping bring domesticated turkeys from the Americas to Europe?
What is the genus name shared by both living turkey species?
The ocellated turkey is notable for what feature compared to the common wild turkey?
choices: Bright pink legs and no tail
Eye-like spots on its tail feathers

questions.q: Domesticated turkeys raised for meat, such as the Broad Breasted White, generally cannot do what?
What is the term for the period when young turkeys are raised by and stay close to the hen?
Turkey eggs are typically larger than chicken eggs and are speckled. Roughly how long does it take for a turkey egg to hatch after incubation begins?

Turkeys can change the color of the fleshy bits on their heads, but they cannot see one particular color especially well at night. Compared with humans, a turkey's daytime color vision is enhanced by having how many types of color-detecting cone cells?
